mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are followed by broad market gains and rejected when equities fall despite easing.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month falling FEDFUNDS trend, or rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates above the policy rate, real rates fall and investors price a faster pivot to easier policy, supporting equity returns over the next week. The mechanism fails if high inflation is accompanied by persistent equity weakness rather than relief buying.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation running above FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances deliver negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the CPI minus FEDFUNDS spread has no positive conditional association with returns in any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening payroll income and slower future demand, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should strengthen when UNRATE rises together with PAYEMS stagnation.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of UNRATE acceleration episodes, or if the signal loses sign consistency when payroll growth is flat or negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales signals stronger household demand and revenue growth for cyclicals, supporting S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The claim is falsified if sales strength does not translate into positive equity performance after the lagged earnings window.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with rising RSAFS on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production indicates improving real activity and capacity utilization, which raises expected earnings and supports S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal should fail when production gains are purely inventory-driven and not accompanied by market advances.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months with positive 3-month INDPRO momentum, or if the predictive relationship disappears after excluding recession exits.

mechanism_narrative: A pickup in housing starts reflects easier financing and stronger domestic demand, which improves cyclical growth expectations and lifts equities over the next quarter. The mechanism is invalid when housing strength is offset by broader macro contraction or tightening credit conditions.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% over 2 months while FEDFUNDS is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered observations, or if the housing-growth signal loses significance when FEDFUNDS is not easing.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate indicates a still-tight labor market, sustaining income growth and consumer spending and supporting near-term equity returns. The signal weakens if tight labor conditions are accompanied by accelerating inflation and aggressive policy tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months with UNRATE below 4.8%, or if the effect vanishes once inflation is controlled for in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in GDP and retail sales marks synchronized demand expansion, which should increase earnings estimates and push the S&P 500 higher over the next month. The claim is falsified if growth in both series does not improve equity returns relative to either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth is not positive in out-of-sample tests, or if triggered combinations do not beat single-series signals over any 3-year rolling window.
